question: Will Australia win Group D in the 2026 FIFA World Cup? Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://polymarket.com/market/will-australia-win-group-d-in-the-2026-fifa-world-cup. Background: This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group D in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026. If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”. The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.content: ### Back Where We Belong Australia's path to South Africa 2010 was through Asia for the first time. It meant that instead of relying on a one-off two-legged playoff once every four years, the Qantas Socceroos had to go through two group stages of Asian qualifying. The Qantas Socceroos got through a tricky opening group relatively comfortably, qualifying for the next stage along with Qatar. The second group stage featured two groups of five nations, with the top two from each qualifying. Australia received a kind draw with only one other country in its group, Japan, having made the World Cup before. Wins away to Uzbekistan and home to Qatar put Australia in a strong position and when it drew 0-0 with Japan in Yokohama, they were in pole position to go through. evidences.content: Teams eliminated Co-hosts Canada, Mexico and USA all receive automatic berths, leaving three direct slots and a possible further two via the Play-off Tournament up for grabs in Concacaf. The qualification tournament began in March 2024, with the region's four lowest-ranked teams meeting in two-legged ties and the winners advancing. The second qualification round ran from June 2024 to June 2025 where 30 Concacaf sides did battle in six groups of five teams. The group winners and runners-up (12 teams in total) progressed to the third and final round. That dozen were split into three groups of four teams. Within each group, teams played each other home and away. The three group winners qualified directly for the World Cup, joining hosts Canada, Mexico and USA.
